subject: Mozi Minute: The Unknown Talent Ratio
body:
Words I like: Hiring talented people can be one of the easiest 10x-100x ROI opportunities. And there's always underpriced talent.

Mozi Minute: LTGPE:CAT

Hypothetically, would you pay $50,000 to make $400,000?

Of course. It's an 8x return.

But most business owners won't do this when it comes to hiring.

They'll complain about not being able to find talent.

They'll say "good people don't exist anymore."

But they're just not paying enough.

Example:

A business needs a 6 figure role.

Someone who would make them multiple six figures in profit.

Every year.

But they only spend a few thousand dollars and make an indeed post to hire this person.

People will spend $10,000 on Facebook ads to get customers at a 3x return.

But won't spend $10,000 to find someone who's going to make them $100,000 every year.

The problem is most people have no idea what an employee is actually worth to them.

So they do what everyone else does.

And what everyone else does doesn't work.

That's why everyone complains about hiring.

Here's the fix:

LTGPE:CAT

Lifetime Gross Profit Per Employee : Cost to Acquire Talent

This equation tells you exactly how much an employee is worth.

Once you know that number, you can spend the right amount to get them.

How to calculate it:

- Figure out how much gross profit a role brings into your business every year.
- Subtract what you pay that person.

That's your gross profit per employee per year.

Now if you knew you'd make that much profit, how much would you be willing to spend to get it?

That's your cost to acquire talent.

Hypothetical Example:

Let's say you own a dental practice.

You need to hire a doctor.

This doctor can do $500,000 worth of work every year.

And you pay the doctor $100,000 annually.

So every year, this one employee makes you $400,000 in gross profit.

Your equation is: $400,000 : whatever you're willing to spend

If someone walked up to you and said...

"Give me $50,000 and I'll give you back $400,000 in twelve months"

Would you do it?

Of course.

That's an 8:1 return. In year one.

But most dental practices?

They're spending maybe $5,000 to hire a doctor.

That's why they can't find talent.

Proof:

A friend of mine owns an insurance agency.

He was struggling to hire sales reps.

And he was paying a $500 bonus to his employee to incentivize referrals.

No one gave any.

Once he figured out that each rep made him $150,000 in gross profit in 6 months.

He changed his bonus for his employees from $500 to $25,000.

Guess what happened?

He no longer had hiring issues.

Why this works:

Good people exist.

But good people cost money to find.

You can't get a 10x return by spending like everyone else.

Everyone else is broke. Everyone else can't find talent.

You don't want to do what they do.

You want to spend more than everyone else.

Because when you spend more, you get more.

You get better recruiters. You get better job posts. You get better referrals.

You get access to people who aren't even looking for jobs.

You just have to be willing to make the trade.

As promised, value under a minute.

Pay more for talent,

- Alex
